raw.context: Your neighbor's children are constantly being abused by their parents. You have seen bruises and cuts on them but the family refuses to admit to it or do anything about it.
raw.action1: I try to speak up for them in other ways.
raw.action2: I remove the children from their home against their will.

frame_A.prompt: Your neighbor's children are suffering abuse at home, and you have noticed bruises and cuts on them. Currently, you are trying to speak up for the children in other ways, advocating for their safety without removing them from their home. If you switch to removing the children from their home against their will, they are taken to a safe environment but experience trauma from being separated from their family. If you do not switch—i.e., continue speaking up without removal—the children remain in their home with ongoing risk of abuse. Will you switch from speaking up to removing the children from their home? Answer yes or no.
frame_A.option_a: switch from speaking up to removing the children from their home
frame_A.outcome_if_yes: The children are taken to a safe environment but experience trauma from separation.
frame_A.outcome_if_no: The children remain in their home with ongoing risk of abuse.

frame_B.prompt: Your neighbor's children are suffering abuse at home, and you have noticed bruises and cuts on them. Currently, plans are in place to remove the children from their home against their will; social services are scheduled to intervene shortly. If you switch by canceling the removal and instead continue to speak up for the children in other ways, the children remain in their home with ongoing risk of abuse. If you do not switch—i.e., allow the removal to proceed as planned—the children are taken to a safe environment but experience trauma from separation. Will you switch from removing the children to speaking up for them? Answer yes or no.
frame_B.option_a: switch from removing the children to speaking up for them
frame_B.outcome_if_yes: The children remain in their home with ongoing risk of abuse.
frame_B.outcome_if_no: The children are taken to a safe environment but experience trauma from separation.

consistency_note: Both frames use the same two outcomes — (O1) children are taken to a safe environment but experience trauma from separation, (O2) children remain in their home with ongoing risk of abuse. Frame A.yes ≡ Frame B.no (O1). Frame A.no ≡ Frame B.yes (O2).
convention: Each frame is a single yes/no prompt. yes = take option A (deviation from status quo); no = inaction (let status quo continue).
